# LeetCode Practice Repository

This repository is a recruiter-friendly archive of executable LeetCode work, not a screenshot dump or a notebook of partial ideas. It shows solved problems as runnable Python files, grouped by official LeetCode difficulty so another engineer can quickly see breadth, verification habits, and the kinds of interview problems already shipped.

## Why This Repo Is Worth Reviewing

The strongest signal here is not just volume. It is that the repo keeps solutions inspectable as code, keeps many problems runnable with local harnesses, and now tracks them by official LeetCode difficulty so a recruiter or interviewer can scan from hardest proof down to easier coverage.

## Snapshot Metrics

|---|---:|
| Solved problems | 19 |
| Medium problems | 6 |
| Easy problems | 13 |

## Notable Problems

| 894. All Possible Full Binary Trees | Medium | Dynamic Programming | state modeling and subproblem decomposition |

| 1962. Remove Stones to Minimize the Total | Medium | Heap | priority-queue updates under repeated reductions |
| 71. Simplify Path | Medium | Stack | 0 ms runtime, 100th-percentile result on a Medium stack problem |
| 88. Merge Sorted Array | Easy | Array String | array mutation and pointer coordination |
| 226. Invert Binary Tree | Easy | Binary Tree | tree recursion and traversal correctness |
| 108. Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree | Easy | Divide And Conquer | balanced recursive decomposition |
| 383. Ransom Note | Easy | Hash Map | frequency counting and constant-time lookup reasoning |
| 876. Middle of the Linked List | Easy | Linked List | pointer manipulation and in-place rewiring |
| 977. Squares of a Sorted Array | Easy | Strings | two-pointer and in-place sequence mutation |

## Tech Stack And Why Chosen

- Python 3: concise enough for interview-style implementation work and already aligned with LeetCode's common execution model.
- Plain scripts plus lightweight classes: keeps each solution close to the original platform signature instead of hiding logic inside framework structure.
- Standard-library `unittest`: gives the repo a small regression surface without introducing external dependencies.
- A generated README workflow: turns the repository into a living portfolio surface instead of a stale list that has to be updated by hand.

## Install Or Bootstrap

No package install step is required. Clone the repository and use a local Python 3 interpreter.

```bash
git clone git@github.com:Kevin-Mok/leetcode.git
cd leetcode
python3 --version
```

## Day-To-Day Use

Add a new solution under the matching topic directory, refresh the local LeetCode catalog when you want the full problem list on disk, then regenerate the README so the solved-problem index, difficulty counts, and notable-problem shortlist stay current.

The downloaded catalog lives at `data/leetcode-problem-catalog.json`.

```bash
python3 scripts/download_problem_catalog.py
```

```bash
python3 scripts/update_readme.py
```

Run a solution directly when it has a local harness:

```bash
python3 binary-tree/94-binary-tree-inorder-traversal.py
```

Run the current regression checks:

```bash
python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v
```

## Command Reference

`python3 scripts/update_readme.py`

- Rebuilds the entire root README from tracked problem files and live LeetCode difficulty data.
- No repo-specific flags are required for the default write mode.

`python3 scripts/update_readme.py --check`

- Verifies that `README.md` already matches the generated output.
- Exits nonzero when the README is stale, which makes it suitable for hooks or CI.

`python3 scripts/download_problem_catalog.py`

- Downloads the current LeetCode problem catalog and writes it to `data/leetcode-problem-catalog.json`.
- Supports `--output <path>` if you want to write the catalog somewhere else.

`python3 scripts/install_pre_commit_hook.py`

- Installs a local `pre-commit` hook that reruns the README generator when relevant files are staged.
- No additional flags are required for the default install path.

`python3 <path-to-solution>.py`

- Runs a solution script directly when it includes a local `__main__` harness.
- No repo-specific flags are defined for individual solution scripts.

`python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`

- `-m unittest` runs Python's built-in test runner as a module.
- `discover` finds matching tests automatically.
- `-s tests` limits discovery to the repo's `tests/` directory.
- `-v` prints verbose test names and results.

## Recruiter Notes

This is intentionally not a framework-heavy application. The value is visible problem solving: recursion, dynamic programming, heap operations, pointer rewiring, and small verification loops that show how the code behaves outside the LeetCode UI.
